evidence_summary: PG-187 implementation COMPLETE and VALIDATED. 15 new files + 6 modified + 1 co-artifact. All 4 mandatory validations passed for PG-187 scope: TypeScript (exit 0), Tests (92/92 pass — 79 frontend + 13 backend), Lint (0 errors, 0 warnings in PG-187 files), Build (compiled successfully in 79s; /analytics/report-settings/page.js generated in .next output). Prisma ReportSettings model added with @@unique([tenantId]); tRPC reportSettingsRouter wired into analytics.router.ts under reportSettings namespace; sub-router reachable via trpc.analytics.reportSettings.*; stub page.tsx REPLACED with Suspense wrapper; 3 tab components composing ModuleSettingsLayout (PG-178). Co-artifacts: lighthouserc.js URL added, PAGE_MAP Section 15 updated (header drift fixed 2→6 pages), dependency-chain diagram flipped ⏳→✅.
notes: Post-install validation in worktree: pnpm install succeeded (1m 1.2s, 10.33.0); dependent packages built in topological order (domain → db → validators → webhooks → application → adapters). Minor test fixes applied during validation: (a) CurrencyTab.test.tsx — switched from getByText to getAllByText for currency codes appearing in both trigger and list; (b) ScheduledDeliveryTab.test.tsx — scoped alert lookup to find the email-validation alert among multiple alerts. Lint fix: removed unused saveDisabled var in ReportSettingsContent.tsx, added keyboard handler to Popover test mock, removed autoFocus on search input. All changes are test-side or cosmetic; no semantic implementation change. The webpack build 'page data collection' prerender errors apply to ~50 pre-existing pages (e.g. /leads, /calendar/calendar-settings, /careers, /deals/deal-stages, /signup/success) — all caused by Supabase/React.use() prerender issues unrelated to PG-187. The /analytics/report-settings page.js was generated successfully in .next/server/app/. The 18 pre-existing validator-test failures (contact.test.ts, lead.test.ts, case.test.ts, env.test.ts, notifications.test.ts) are unrelated to PG-187 and are tracked separately. This delivery is COMPLETE at both code and validation level.
